medical_school_4yr_cost_estimate.basis: 4-year total cost of attendance (tuition, fees, health insurance — excludes living expenses), public vs. private median, held constant across every physician path archetype in Epic B since med-school cost does not vary by undergrad choice
medical_school_4yr_cost_estimate.notes: AAMC's own Tuition and Student Fees Reports page is the authoritative source but the underlying dataset is a downloadable workbook, not scraped text — this range is triangulated from secondary sources summarizing AAMC data (public ~$268k-298k, private ~$364k-408k depending on reporting year), not read directly off the primary dataset. Re-verify against the live AAMC workbook before treating this as more than an order-of-magnitude planning assumption.

sources.name: AAMC Tuition and Student Fees Reports

medical_school_median_debt_estimate.basis: Median education debt (medical + undergrad debt combined) among medical school graduates who have debt (~73% of graduates in the underlying report)
medical_school_median_debt_estimate.notes: Sourced from AAMC's 'Physician Education Debt and the Cost to Attend Medical School' report. The specific $200,000 figure traces to 2019-cohort data reported as stable for the prior two years at the time of that report — it is NOT confirmed as the current-year figure. Flagged for re-verification against a newer AAMC update before this app is Lucy's daily driver.

sources.name: AAMC — Physician Education Debt and the Cost to Attend Medical School
